title: Slim consciousness.txt + make capability claims structurally un-driftable
current_state: consciousness.txt is 426 lines (three docs claim ~700 — even the docs about the prompt drift). It hand-lists behaviors and capabilities that the consciousness-sync passive skill then has to police after the fact. Identity and capability-inventory are tangled in one hand-edited file.
target_state: consciousness.txt = identity + values + honesty rules ONLY (~250 lines). Capability inventory becomes an auto-generated block (from the live tool registry, same source as PI.md §7) injected via the existing {{INCLUDE:}} mechanism or a marker section refreshed by refresh_pi.py. verify.py gains a cheap check: every tool name mentioned in consciousness.txt exists in the registry — a claim about a nonexistent tool FAILS the build. The LOG1/LOG2 anti-miming lesson, enforced by CI instead of vigilance.
migration_plan: 1. Audit current 426 lines: classify identity / capability-claim / dead.
2. Move capability text into an AUTO block generated from get_tool_definitions() (grouped, one line per group — keep prompt tokens flat).
4. verify.py check: regex tool-name mentions against registry names; unknown → FAIL with the offending line.
5. Re-run prompt-cache split (static segment changes) and confirm cache hit behavior unchanged.
risk_notes: consciousness.txt + prompt.py are RISK_FLAGGED → interactive, diff-first. Behavior change risk: trim conservatively, never reword the honesty/anti-miming sections.
